paper_id: Pinheiro et al. 2024 - Advancing Toward a Reference Ontology for Enterprise Architecture Mining from APIs
input_limitations: The Markdown extraction contains encoding artifacts and omitted figure content, but the main text sections needed for coding are readable.

models.model_label: Reference ontology for EA mining from APIs

evidence_quotes.quote: This paper presents an extensible ontology for EA mining to extract models using Application Program Interface (API) log files as the data source.
The ontology under development aims to extract data correlating different API calls to build views of the AS-IS EA model by mining data from API logs, namely, from a centralized log of API Gateways.
It will help to automate the EA modeling of the AS-IS architecture and make it more reliable and faster.

goals.justification: The ontology is explicitly framed as an instrument for extracting and building EA model views from API logs. This fits Intervening because the model enables artifact generation and automation of EA modeling.

evidence_quotes.quote: For this, it requires developing an ontology to clarify and explain the process and drive the main concepts and the sequence of steps to build a concise solution design for the context under analysis.
The novelty of our proposed ontology is the ability to correlate different requests that initially have no physical correlations but share logical contexts relevant to understanding the EA.

goals.justification: The authors state that the ontology clarifies and explains the process and main concepts, and that its correlations are relevant to understanding enterprise architecture. This directly matches the Understanding goal.

evidence_quotes.quote: Theapplication of the ontology is an adequate approach for automating the capture of an EA model evolution, which leverages the tracking of change in the current EA model and provides views to aid the impact analysis of planning changes in enterprise APIs, and, finally, for monitoring the effects of the architectural change's implementation, contributing to an agile EA management.

goals.justification: The ontology-generated views are described as aiding impact analysis of planned API changes and monitoring change effects. This supports Problem-solving, although the link is through the produced views rather than a detailed decision procedure.

evidence_quotes.quote: In addition, this work evolves the ontology to follow the FAIR principles (Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability, and Reusability) to facilitate its publication in the FAIR multi-domain catalog for Ontology-Driven Conceptual Modeling 3 and promote its reuse and extension to other cases. [29].

According to Falbo [10], reference domain ontologies must approximate the ideal ontology of the domain as well as possible, focusing on the representation adequacy of the resulting specifications targeting its use by humans.

goals.justification: The ontology is framed as a reference artifact intended for publication, reuse, extension, and human use. This indicates an asynchronous reference/documentation function, though it is secondary to extraction and conceptualization.
